fields.bio: Akmalkhoja Yusupovich Mavlonov (born May 12, 1975, Quva District, Fergana Region, Uzbek SSR) is an Uzbek statesman, Major General of the Customs Service of the Republic of Uzbekistan, as well as a legal expert and economist. Since 2021, he has been serving as the Chairman of the Customs Committee of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
fields.bio_en: Akmalkhoja Yusupovich Mavlonov (born May 12, 1975, Quva District, Fergana Region, Uzbek SSR) is an Uzbek statesman, Major General of the Customs Service of the Republic of Uzbekistan, as well as a legal expert and economist. Since 2021, he has been serving as the Chairman of the Customs Committee of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

Since 2021, he has been serving as the Chairman of the Customs Committee of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

fields.name: Ian Saunders
fields.title: Secretary General
fields.bio:
Ian Saunders is an American customs and trade policy leader who became Secretary General of the World Customs Organization (WCO) in January 2024. With more than three decades in international relations, he is known for advancing global customs modernization and promoting inclusive, rules-based trade cooperation.

Key facts
Position: Secretary General, World Customs Organization (since 2024)

Nationality: United States

Education: Georgetown University; executive programs at Harvard Kennedy School and American University

Prior roles: Deputy Assistant Secretary, U.S. Department of Commerce; senior executive, U.S. Customs and Border Protection

Experience: 30+ years in customs, trade, and international policy

Career and background
Before his election, Saunders served as Deputy Assistant Secretary for the Western Hemisphere at the U.S. Department of CommerceŌĆÖs International Trade Administration. There, he led programs and strategies to strengthen U.S. commercial partnerships across the Americas. Earlier, at U.S. Customs and Border Protection, he held senior posts including Assistant Commissioner for International Affairs and Acting Deputy Executive Assistant Commissioner for Operations Support. He joined the U.S. Senior Executive Service in 2008. 

Leadership at the World Customs Organization
Elected in June 2023 and assuming office in January 2024, Saunders heads the WCOŌĆÖs efforts to secure and facilitate global trade, protect revenue, and modernize customs operations worldwide. His priorities include balancing tradition with innovation, promoting transparency and inclusivity, and strengthening global supply-chain resilience amid climate, health, and digital challenges. 

Vision and impact
Saunders emphasizes cooperative border management, technological innovation, and diverse participation across the WCOŌĆÖs 185 member administrations. Drawing on his long-standing work on frameworks such as the SAFE Framework of Standards, he advocates pragmatic governance that aligns customs modernization with security and sustainable development goals.

fields.description: An authorized law enforcement body in the field of customs administration under the Ministry of Finance of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Its main functions include ensuring compliance with customs legislation, maintaining statistics on foreign trade and cross-border movement of goods, and combating smuggling.

fields.name: The National Canine Center of the State Customs Committee
fields.name_en: The National Canine Center of the State Customs Committee
fields.name_fr: The National Canine Center of the State Customs Committee
fields.description:
The canine service of the State Customs Committee was established in October 1993. The National Canine Center, founded on October 24, 1996, by a resolution of the Cabinet of Ministers, serves as the main institution for training canine specialists and service dogs.**

The Center provides methodological support to customs and other law enforcement agencies, conducts initial and advanced training for canine handlers, and is responsible for breeding, selecting, and training service dogs.

Located in the Sergeli district of Tashkent, the Center features modern facilities, including training grounds, a kennel, a veterinary unit, and educational departments equipped for both theoretical and practical instruction.

Service dogs are trained in key areas such as narcotics detection, explosives and weapons detection, counter-terrorism, tracking, protection, and search-and-rescue operations. Work is also underway to train dogs in detecting foreign currency.

The Center maintains an automated information system for monitoring canine operations and uses electronic microchipping to manage detailed data on each service dog.

Its specialists regularly exchange experience with international partners, and the Center also provides training for foreign canine service representatives. To date, more than 30 training manuals have been developed.

In 2008, the Center was granted the status of a Regional Canine Center by the World Customs Organization, confirming its compliance with international standards. Its activities have been highly evaluated by international experts.

Service dogs trained at the Center have played a significant role in detecting illegal trafficking of narcotics, psychotropic substances, weapons, and ammunition.

fields.name: Customs Institute
Customs border post
fields.description:
A customs post is a unit of the customs authority empowered to carry out full-scale customs clearance and customs control at a specific location or within a designated area. It does not have the status of a legal entity.

A customs post is usually located away from the main customs administration and operates within the jurisdiction of a subordinate customs authority. Its activities typically cover one or two districts of a region, as well as transport infrastructure facilities such as small river ports, airports, and railway stations. Customs posts are established at nearly every border crossing along the state frontier. They may also be set up within large industrial enterprises.

A customs post is primarily responsible for the inspection and clearance of goods.

The structure of a customs post includes the head of the post and divisions organized on a territorial basis. The divisionsŌĆÖ staff typically consists of a division head, a senior customs service inspector, senior inspectors, and inspectors.

fields.title: Center for Islamic Civilization
fields.description:
The Center for Islamic Civilization in Uzbekistan under the Cabinet of Ministers of the Republic of Uzbekistan (Arabic: ┘ģž▒┘āž▓ ž¦┘äžŁžČž¦ž▒ž® ž¦┘äžźž│┘äž¦┘ģ┘Ŗž® ┘ü┘Ŗ žŻ┘łž▓ž©┘āž│ž¬ž¦┘å ┘äž»┘ē ┘ģž¼┘äž│ ž¦┘ä┘łž▓ž▒ž¦žĪ ┘äž¼┘ģ┘ć┘łž▒┘Ŗž® žŻ┘łž▓ž©┘āž│ž¬ž¦┘å; English: Center of Islamic Civilization in Uzbekistan under the Cabinet of Ministers of the Republic of Uzbekistan) is a research institution in Uzbekistan engaged in scientific studies related to Islamic history.

On March 17, 2026, under the leadership of Shavkat Mirziyoyev, on the eve of Eid al-Fitr, the official opening ceremony of the Center was held. On April 13, 2026, it was included in the Guinness World Records in the category of ŌĆ£the largest Islamic civilization museum.ŌĆØ

History

The Center for Islamic Civilization in Uzbekistan was established in accordance with the Presidential Decree of the Republic of Uzbekistan dated July 23, 2017, issued by Shavkat Mirziyoyev. Over more than eight years, this large complex was constructed and fully completed. On March 17, 2026, on the eve of Eid al-Fitr, the Center was officially inaugurated.

fields.title: State Museum of the Temurids
fields.description:
The State Museum of the History of the Timurids is a museum located in the center of Tashkent dedicated to the history of Central Asia during the era of Amir Temur and the dynasty he founded. The museum is included in the ŌĆ£List of State Unique Scientific ObjectsŌĆØ of the Republic of Uzbekistan. It houses more than 5,000 exhibits related to the period of Timur and the Timurid dynasty.

The museum was built on October 18, 1996, in connection with the 660th anniversary of Amir Temur. It is 31 meters high, with a diameter of 70 meters, and contains 500 lighting units.

The museum consists of three floors.

History

The State Museum of the History of the Timurids was established on the initiative of the First President of the Republic of Uzbekistan, Islam Karimov, in 1996 to commemorate the 660th anniversary of Amir TemurŌĆÖs birth. In 2006, on the occasion of the museumŌĆÖs 10th anniversary, the exhibition ŌĆ£10 Years of the State Museum of the History of the TimuridsŌĆØ was opened.

Between 2007 and 2011, a fundamental research project titled ŌĆ£The Study of Written Monuments of the Timurid Period in Uzbekistan and AbroadŌĆØ was implemented. Within the framework of this project, materials and information related to manuscripts from the era of Amir Temur and the Timurids were collected.

fields.title: Tashkent Television Tower
fields.description: Tashkent Television Tower is the tallest structure in Central Asia and one of the main landmarks of Tashkent. Built in 1985, the tower stands 375 meters high and serves as a broadcasting center for television and radio signals. It also features an observation deck offering panoramic views of the city, making it a popular attraction for visitors and official guests.

fields.title: New Uzbekistan Park
fields.description: New Uzbekistan Park is one of TashkentŌĆÖs largest and most modern parks, symbolizing the countryŌĆÖs renewal and future development. The park features vast green areas, fountains, wide walking paths, and a central monument representing national unity and progress. It is a popular destination for residents and visitors, offering a peaceful setting for recreation, cultural events, and official visits.

fields.subtitle: Conference Venue ŌĆō Grand Ballroom
fields.description:
The Grand Ballroom at JW Marriott Hotel Tashkent offers a sophisticated and spacious setting for high-level conferences, meetings, and official events in the heart of Tashkent City. With approximately 820 m┬▓ of pillar-free space and a ceiling height of around 8.5 meters, the venue provides an elegant and flexible environment suitable for a wide range of event formats. (Marriott)
The ballroom can accommodate up to 600 participants in theatre style, 450 for banquets, and 700 guests for receptions, making it ideal for large-scale international conferences and corporate gatherings. (Marriott)
Equipped with modern audio-visual technology, on-site technical support, and adjacent pre-function areas for registration and networking, the Grand Ballroom ensures a seamless and professional event experience in one of the cityŌĆÖs leading luxury hotels.
